strengths: Eight distinct pages with clearly differentiated layout patterns (zine cover, asymmetric grid, vinyl flip catalog, typewriter tour list, contact-sheet merch, foldout history)
Tight alignment with the seed: vinyl mockups, sticky audio player, tour list, merch shop, history zine page all present
Concrete motif execution — masking tape corners, halftone portraits, riso misregistration, marker scrawls, stencil type — not generic
Rich interactions: click-to-flip sleeves, B-side reveal, genre chip filtering, tear-off newsletter strip, queue popout in player
Specific content model with catalog numbers (XRX-001 to XRX-047), pressing variants, credits ledger
weaknesses: Roster card 'hear them' play interaction overlaps slightly with release-detail play behavior
History page chapter dates are plausibly fine but could risk feeling like a template timeline if not visually distinct

strengths: Nine pages, all visually and functionally differentiated
Distinctive devices: cassette J-card fold-open, tide-line horizontal tour timeline, cassette-deck VU player bar, tape-club countdown
Strong regional flavor (Olympia/Oakland, PNW chips, hand-dubbed editions) avoids generic indie-label feel
Adds tape-club subscription page and reading-room essay index that go beyond the seed without drifting
weaknesses: Seed explicitly calls out 'vinyl mockups' as central; this concept leans cassette-first, making the vinyl emphasis secondary
Tide-line metaphor for tour dates is creative but slightly less legible than a straight chronological list for booker/promoter audience
Shop page is the least differentiated relative to candidate 1
